tool_args.label: Capital Program Committee Meeting Book - September 2023

payload.title: Capital Program Committee Meeting
payload.description: Capital Program Committee meeting materials including minutes from July 17, 2023 meeting, Capital Program Committee Work Plan, Stations projects update, IEC review, Safety Summary, Commitments & Completions, 2nd Quarter 2023 Traffic Light Report, and procurement actions for September 2023.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Parent transportation authority overseeing MTA Construction & Development, NYCT, LIRR, MNR, B&T and other agencies.
Construction and development arm of the MTA responsible for capital program delivery.
Independent Engineering Consultant (IEC) providing project review for MTA Capital Program.
MTA Board committee responsible for oversight of the capital program.

tool_args.label: Independent Engineering Consultant (IEC) - Michael Baker International

payload.description: The IEC monitors the Stations Business Unit through cost and schedule analysis of projects published in the quarterly Traffic Light Report and through risk-based monitoring.

warnings: entity.organization is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Organization names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: part_of_agency.

tool_args.label: ADA Upgrades at 149 St-Grand Concourse and Tremont Av

payload.project_name: ADA Upgrades at 149 St-Grand Concourse and Tremont Av
payload.description: Provides ADA accessibility to three underground subway stations in the Bronx: the 149 St-Grand Concourse complex (2, 4, 5) and Tremont Av (B, D). Includes installation of six new elevators, stairs, elevator machine rooms, and ADA boarding areas. Budget $163M. Design-build contractor: Tully Construction.

Design-build contract to provide ADA accessibility at 68 St / Hunter College (6). Includes three new elevators, two new street to platform entrances, utility replacement, mezzanine expansion, track replacement work, and state of good repair work. Design-build JV: Forte and Citnalta. Budget $177M.

tool_args.label: ADA Package 1: Accessibility Upgrade at Eight Stations

payload.project_name: ADA Package 1: Accessibility Upgrade at Eight Stations
payload.description: Provides ADA accessibility at eight stations: New Dorp (SIR), Grand St (L), Beach 67 St (A), 7 Av (F, G), Dyckman St northbound only (1), East 149 St (6), Lorimer St (L), and Metropolitan Av (G). Design-build contractor: Judlau Contracting. 18 elevators bundled. Budget reduced from $324M to $264M.

tool_args.label: ADA Package 2: Accessibility Upgrade, Elevator Replacement, and Station Improvements at Eight Stations

payload.project_name: ADA Package 2: Accessibility Upgrade, Elevator Replacement, and Station Improvements at Eight Stations
payload.description: New ADA compliant elevators at six stations and replacement elevators at two stations. New ADA at 8 Av southbound (N), Westchester Sq (6), 181 St (A), Woodhaven Blvd (J/Z), Court Square (G), Queensboro Plaza (7/N/W). Replacement at Marcy Av (J/Z) and Flushing Avenue (J/Z). Design-Builder: MLJTC2 JV (MLJ Contracting and TC Electric). Budget $471M.
C&D's first Public-Private Partnership. 23 new and 14 elevator replacements at 13 stations. Contractor/DB JV: EAE (Halmar/Forte). Budget $692M. Awarded December 2022.
8 new elevators and 1 escalator at 4 stations. Contractor/DB JV: JTTC/AECOM. Budget $237M. Awarded December 2022.
3 new elevators at Borough Hall station. Contractor/DB JV: Judlau/TyLin. Budget $175M. Awarded December 2022.
Repairs and state of good repair work (no elevator work) at 5 stations on the Flushing Line. Contractor: Skanska/RR JV Judlau. Budget $623M. Awarded September 2022.

Provides new MNR rail service to and from Penn Station, delivering four new ADA stations in the Bronx, reducing commuting times by up to 50 minutes each way. 19 miles of new track, over 200 new catenary structures, 8 power substations, rehabilitation of 4 bridges. Budget $2.8B.
Capital Program Committee Meeting held on July 17, 2023 at 2:12 PM. Minutes approved for September 2023 meeting.
Capital Program Committee Meeting scheduled for September 18, 2023, 12:45-2:00 PM ET at 2 Broadway, 20th Floor Board Room, New York, NY 10004.
In 2023 the MTA planned to commit $10 billion worth of capital projects.
Through August, the MTA has committed $2.372 billion versus a $5.521 billion YTD goal.
ADA Package 1 budget reduced from $324M to $264M.
The overall value of the Stations Program represented in the TLR is $10.2 B.

payload.raw_value_text: 518 Projects were Reviewed for the 2nd Quarter 2023
payload.description: A total of 518 Projects were Reviewed for the 2nd Quarter 2023 Traffic Light Report.

tool_args.label: 35 Lost Time Incidents YTD through August 2023

payload.raw_value_text: 35 Lost Time incidents have been reported YTD (through August 31, 2023)
payload.description: 35 Lost Time incidents YTD, a decrease of 20% vs same period in 2022.
Bundling 18 elevators (12 machine-room-less and 6 hydraulic) across eight stations into one contract. Design-build contractor: Judlau Contracting.
Eleven new and five replacement elevators (16 total). Design-Builder: MLJTC2 JV (MLJ Contracting and TC Electric) with AECOM as Lead Designer.

payload.subject: Station projects in TLR
payload.description: Of 108 station projects monitored, 90 (83%) were designated GREEN, 5 (5%) RED, and 13 (12%) triggered a variance.

payload.claim_text: The following Station Packages have been awarded since 2020. They will provide 92 new elevators at 38 locations, providing full accessibility at these stations by 2026.
payload.subject: ADA station packages
payload.description: Seven ADA station packages awarded since 2020 will provide 92 new elevators at 38 locations, providing full accessibility at these stations by 2026.

payload.claim_text: For the first time ever, the MTA is prioritizing accessibility, constructing more elevators and making more spaces accessible than the three previous Capital Plans combined.
payload.subject: MTA accessibility program
payload.description: Statement by Quemuel Arroyo that MTA is prioritizing accessibility more than the three previous Capital Plans combined.

tool_args.label: Penn Station Access - implemented by MTA C&D

payload.description: MTA Construction & Development implements the Metro-North Penn Station Access Project
ADA Package 1 includes installation of 18 elevators across eight stations
ADA Package 2 includes 11 new and 5 replacement elevators at eight stations
ADA 149th/Tremont is a separate project, not the same as ADA Package 1
ADA 68th St/Hunter College is a separate project with 3 elevators

tool_args.label: Penn Station Access reported at Sept 2023 CPC meeting

payload.description: Penn Station Access project status was reported at the September 2023 Capital Program Committee meeting
